tape: Risk-off undercurrent: US-Iran strikes over the Strait of Hormuz escalated over the weekend and into Monday, Brent crude jumped ~4% to ~$78.82, yet S&P 500 is only -0.3% and Nasdaq -0.7% with VIX still low near 15 - equities largely shrugging off the geopolitical shock so far, but oil-supply risk is live ahead of Tuesday's CPI print.

alerts.headline: US CENTCOM struck Iran targets degrading its ability to hit Strait of Hormuz shipping; Iran retaliated with missile/drone strikes on UAE, Qatar, Kuwait, Oman, Bahrain. Hormuz crossings down sharply, Brent +~4%. Equities so far resilient but this is a live tail risk.
Q2 EPS $0.01 missed the $0.05 estimate (vs $0.12 a year ago), but company advanced a new $100M share buyback (7/9) and insider buying (director Jay Pack) continues; no fresh negative catalyst today.
Stock +14.4% today; no new negative news found - continues to ride H.C. Wainwright's 7/2 Buy initiation and the 6/29 BLA filing/open-label data.

Top radar candidate (score 6.49) still carrying unresolved clinical overhang: Morgan Stanley cut PT to $26 from $55 and H.C. Wainwright downgraded to Neutral from Buy (6/29-6/30) on EVO756 trial data, even though shares are +~4.9% today on no fresh catalyst - stale but material risk the score model may not price in.
Benefiting from the Hormuz-driven oil price surge; Susquehanna's PT cut to $7 (kept Positive) is the only incremental analyst note, otherwise still riding the $1B+ Equinor backlog news.
No fresh news today; background context - the $1.9B Vacuumschmelze cash-and-stock acquisition (announced 6/23, ~65.9M new shares) is already priced in, deal expected to close early 2027.
Steady +~1.9% grind higher, no new catalyst since late-June Guggenheim/H.C. Wainwright PT hikes.
Quiet, +0.6%; earnings scheduled 7/29, no fresh news.
Stable, no fresh news today; continues to hold gains from ICONIQ's insider buying reported earlier in the week.

movers.note: +14.4% today, no fresh catalyst identified beyond existing BLA/coverage momentum.
Lifted by the Hormuz-conflict oil price spike (Brent +~4%).
+~4.9% today despite unresolved late-June clinical downgrades - momentum without a new fundamental driver.
Q2 EPS miss offset by new $100M buyback; roughly flat to slightly down on the session.
